commit.message:
Run real vLLM instances in disaggregated serving demo

Launch two vLLM servers (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct) as Prefill and Decode,
route requests through the proxy, and send test prompts end-to-end.
Demonstrates the full Mooncake proxy pattern with real LLM inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add vLLM disaggregated serving proxy for real Mooncake + vLLM deployment

Implements a simplified version of Mooncake's vllm_v1_proxy_server.py that
routes requests between vLLM Prefill (kv_producer) and Decode (kv_consumer)
instances. Supports round-robin load balancing across multiple nodes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: nano-mooncake — Mooncake minimal prototype in ~650 lines of Python

Implements the core design of Mooncake (FAST 2025 Best Paper) as a teaching prototype:
- Transfer Engine with pluggable TCP transport and batch async transfers
- Mooncake Store with Master/Client, two-phase put, replica tracking, and lease eviction
- HTTP metadata server for segment discovery
- Two runnable examples: Prefill/Decode disaggregation and distributed KVCache store

pull — timing out in CI. Rewrite tests to use version/help/unknown
commands that don't trigger Docker operations.

commit.message:
fix: force-reset _app_tz in conftest to prevent timezone state leakage

The autouse _reset_app_timezone fixture now unconditionally sets
_app_tz = None both before and after each test, instead of saving
and restoring the original value. This prevents timezone state set by
import side-effects or earlier tests from leaking into subsequent tests.

Also adds diagnostic info to test_basic_write to help debug CI-only
timezone failures (will show _app_tz, fallback, and existing files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix file watch behavior to match glob contract (#679)

Fixes a correctness bug where `file-watch-service.ts` watches entire
directory subtrees via `fs.watch` but never filters file change events
against the original glob pattern. Plugins requesting a narrow glob
(e.g. `src/**/*.ts`) would receive events for every file change under
the base directory, regardless of whether the file matched the requested
pattern.

## Changes Made

- **Glob filtering**: Added `picomatch` as a direct dependency and use
it to compile the glob pattern into a matcher. Each file change event is
now tested against the original glob before being queued for forwarding
to plugins.
- **webContentsId tracking**: Added `webContentsId` to `WatchEntry` so
`cleanupWatchesForWindow` correctly stops only watches belonging to the
closed window instead of using no-op void stubs.
- **Destroyed sender guard**: Added `sender.isDestroyed()` check before
sending batched events, with automatic watch cleanup on failure.
- **Windows path support**: `extractBaseDir` now normalizes backslashes
to forward slashes for cross-platform glob handling.
- **Tests**: Added comprehensive unit tests in
`file-watch-service.test.ts` covering glob filtering, lifecycle
management, per-window cleanup, debounce behavior, and edge cases (14
tests).

## Files Changed

- `src/main/services/file-watch-service.ts` — Import `picomatch` and
apply glob filter on file events before forwarding; add webContentsId
tracking, isDestroyed guard, and Windows path normalization
- `src/main/services/file-watch-service.test.ts` — New test file with 14
tests for glob filtering and service behavior
- `package.json` / `package-lock.json` — Added `picomatch` and
`@types/picomatch` dependencies

## Testing

- ✅ All 14 unit tests pass
- ✅ TypeScript type checking passes
- ✅ CodeQL security scan clean

> Correctness bug and performance issue -- every change under the tree
wakes plugin code, regardless of whether the file matches the requested
glob pattern.

> Apply the original glob pattern as a filter on file change events
before forwarding them to plugins. Use a library like `minimatch` or
`picomatch` to test each changed path against the
glob.</issue_description>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(renderer): remove window.renderFrame stub that shadowed babulus-standard.js

The stub was injected into the HTML before browser bundles loaded, which
prevented babulus-standard.js from providing its real ComposableRenderer-based
implementation. browser-components.js also has an if (!window.renderFrame)
guard so it never overrode the stub either. Result: all rendered frames were
blank white. Removing the stub lets babulus-standard.js own renderFrame
exclusively as intended.

commit.message:
Upgrade to mdbr-leaf-ir model, expand semantic presets to 160 items

Replace all-MiniLM-L6-v2 with MongoDB's mdbr-leaf-ir (#1 retrieval
model under 100M params on MTEB). 768 dims, q4f16 quantization, ~30MB
download. Significantly better search quality.

Expand semantic presets from 3x15 to 5 categories with 160 total items:
- Knowledge (40): science, history, geography, technology
- Products (30): electronics, fitness, kitchen, outdoor gear
- FAQ (30): customer support questions
- Movie Plots (30): plot summaries for semantic film search
- Recipes (30): dishes from around the world

Fix missing entities by removing blocking first-refresh from setup

The AIS WebSocket fetch can take up to 90s (waiting for a position
report), which exceeds HA's 60s integration setup timeout.  This caused
setup to be killed before platforms were loaded, resulting in zero
entities.

Now platforms are set up immediately and the first data fetch runs in
the background.  Entities show "Unknown" until the first AIS report
arrives.

feat: v1.0.15 — application/ layer extraction (Clean Architecture)

Extract application/ layer following Clean Architecture's dependency rule:
  domain/ ← application/ ← infrastructure/

New package: src/tmux_orchestrator/application/
  - supervision.py    — supervised_task (Erlang OTP Supervisor Pattern)
  - workflow_service.py — Workflow, WorkflowStep, TaskSubmitter Protocol, _topological_sort
  - monitor_protocols.py — ContextMonitorProtocol, DriftMonitorProtocol, NullContextMonitor, NullDriftMonitor
  - __init__.py        — re-exports all public symbols

Strangler Fig shims (old paths continue to work):
  - supervision.py  → re-exports from application.supervision
  - workflow.py     → re-exports from application.workflow_service
  - orchestrator.py → imports protocols from application.monitor_protocols, re-exports them

Layer purity enforced by:
  - tests/test_application_purity.py (18 tests): AST import graph checks
  - Identity tests confirm shim objects === application/ objects

Total tests: 1227 (18 new). All passing.
Demo: Pipeline pattern with 2 real agents (Stack impl + verification). 21/21 checks PASSED.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix AdminPanel JSX parse errors blocking all Vercel builds Bounties section had extra orphaned , governance section was missing closing for its space-y-16 wrapper, and both inline conditional renders used `&&
` pattern without parens causing Turbopack to misparse the closing tags as regexp literals. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
Fix role/status case mismatch causing students and instructors to show 0

Root cause: signup and invite routes were inserting role='STUDENT'/'INSTRUCTOR'
and status='PENDING' (uppercase) while the DB schema and queries expect
lowercase. This caused .eq('role','student') to match nothing.

Fixes:
- signup/actions.ts: role='student', status='pending'
- invite/route.ts: role='instructor', status='active', profile_status='active'
- dashboard-stats API: use .ilike() for role filter to catch any existing
  uppercase records already in the database

the iframe. Override backgroundPosition to 'center 80%' to shift the
image up, cropping the white area entirely.

chat model by compressing conversation history each turn. Tested with

commit.message:
fix(installer): move source detection before set -e

The previous fix placed the source guard after `set -e`, so sourcing
the script still applied `set -e` to the user's shell. On a second run
or any subsequent failing command, the shell would exit and close the
terminal window. Moving the guard before `set -e` prevents this.

commit.message:
Add fighter experience filter and auto-detect UFC debuts

Skip betting on fights where either fighter has fewer than 3 UFC fights
(configurable via MIN_FIGHTER_FIGHTS). Debutants and low-experience
fighters have unreliable rolling stats (~67% NaN), so the model's
contribution is mostly noise for these fights.

Changes:
- config.py: Add MIN_FIGHTER_FIGHTS = 3 setting
- build_features.py: Add get_fighter_ufc_fight_count() lookup function
- value.py: Add experience filter to _passes_filters() and find_value_bets()
- backtest.py: Pass fighter experience through filters in both standard
  and walk-forward backtests
- bot.py: Auto-detect debuts in predict and live commands, show [LOW EXP]
  warnings and skip low-experience fights in live execution

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

actual product journey: run context setup, fill out a Context Brief in
your browser, pipeline auto-runs (introspect → scaffold → enrich →
verify), curate to Gold in the visual studio, serve via MCP. Bump to
v0.5.2.

commit.message:
Fix "No Response" caused by unreliable device_status_v2 offline detection

Remove checkDeviceOnline() from characteristic getters — the
device_status_v2 streaming event was falsely marking devices offline,
causing all getters to throw unhandled errors and HomeKit to show
"No Response" / "Updating" for all thermostats.

Getters now always return cached data or sensible defaults, which is
the expected pattern for HomeKit characteristic handlers.
